questions: What are the six bot bugs? No notes file is attached to the plan directory — please paste the list (ideally with file:line references and a one-line description of each symptom).
Where should I look for the notes if they already exist somewhere? (e.g., a GitHub issue, a Discord message, a local file outside `.megaplan/`, the `docs/` directory?)
For each bug, do you already have a preferred fix in mind, or should I propose one per bug before implementing?
Should fixes be landed as one bundled commit or six separate commits (one per bug)?
Are there any of the six bugs you'd like me to skip or defer (e.g., ones that need product input)?
The working tree has unrelated in-progress changes in `.megaplan/schemas/*.json` and `src/features/summarising/summariser.py` — should I leave those alone, or are any of them related to the six bugs?

success_criteria.criterion: The six specific bugs from the user's notes are each addressed by a minimal, targeted code change
Every touched Python file passes `python -m py_compile` cleanly
Every touched module imports without error from the repo root
No unrelated files are modified (working-tree changes in `.megaplan/schemas/*.json` and `src/features/summarising/summariser.py` remain untouched unless they are one of the six bugs)
Any existing tests that cover the touched modules still pass
Each fix is the smallest viable change — no refactors, no defensive wrappers, no drive-by cleanups
Each bug fix is isolated enough to be reverted independently (ideally one commit per bug)
Manual smoke in dev Discord bot confirms the fixed behaviors for any bugs whose symptoms can only be observed at runtime

assumptions: 'Bot' refers to the Discord bot in this repo (`brain-of-bndc`, entry point `main.py`, features under `src/features/`).
'Light cleanup' means each of the six bugs is small enough to fix in a handful of lines without refactoring — if any turns out to be larger, I will stop and re-plan rather than expand scope.

'See notes' refers to a list the user will paste in response to the clarifying questions; no pre-existing notes file has been located in `.megaplan/plans/light-cleanup-of-six-bot-bugs-20260408-0250/` or elsewhere obvious.
The in-progress modifications in `.megaplan/schemas/*.json` and `src/features/summarising/summariser.py` are unrelated to the six bugs and should be left alone.
Fixes will be landed as six separate commits on `main` (current branch) unless the user says otherwise.
No new dependencies, no new files (unless a bug specifically requires one), no test infrastructure changes.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
